rulings.Case Title: GELAREH RAHBAR DDS VS. JENNIFER BATOON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Motion For Atty'S Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, November 7, 2011, Line 6. DEFENDANT JENNIFER BATOON Motion For Attorney's Fees And Costs. Granted in part. Defendant is awarded $4776 as reasonable fees and costs, calculated at $150 per hour for 30 hours and $276 in costs. 30 hours is a reasonable number of hours for the work performed. $150 per hour is a reasonable hourly rate for routine collection work. 30 hours is a reasonable number of hours for the work performed. If defendant seeks any further fees and costs, she needs to show that she made a reasonable settlement demand consistent with plaintiff¿s documented ability to pay and that the demand was rejected. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: RUBEN RAMIREZ et al VS. ESTATE OF JUAN MARTINEZ MAGALLAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Change Of Venue
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, November 7, 2011, Line 7. DEFENDANT ROGER HAKKER, CHERYL HAKKER, SIMON HAKKER, RHONDA HAKKER Motion For Change Of Venue. Denied. Moving parties did not file their motion within a reasonable time after they filed their answer on December 23, 2010. (Weil & Brown 3:567-68). Notice of the trial date was sent by the court on April 1, 2011. To grant the motion now would unfairly lengthen the duration of this case to the prejudice of the plaintiffs. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MONICA N GANJOO et al VS. JEFF GRIFFITHS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Amended Special Interrogatories (Set One); Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Deft And His Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 7, 2011, Line 3. PLAINTIFFS MONICA GANJOO AND GANJOO LAW OFFICES' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO AMENDED SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES (SET ONE) AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFT AND HIS COUNSEL. Granted as to interrogatories 1 and 5 and denied as to interrogatory 2 Sanctions are denied. If defendant has any further responsive information to interrogatories 1 and 5, he needs to provide it without hiding behind non-meritorious objections. If he doesn¿t, he needs to explicitly so state and/or remove his objections. The response to interrogatory 2 is full and non-evasive. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CROWN BUILDING MAINTENANCE, INC., A BUILDING VS. SBM SITE SERVICES, LLC, AN OREGON LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 7, 2011, Line 9. CROSS DEFENDANTS MARK KELLY, RICHARD MULKERRINS, MICHAEL MCGEE AND CROWN BUILDING MAINTENANCE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Two demurrers were filed. This is a combined ruling that applies to both. Overruled as to the first, second, and fourth causes of action, sustained without leave to amend as to the third and fifth causes of action, and ten days leave to amend to allege a conversion claim. The UCL cause of action pleads acts outside the preemptive scope of the UTSA, the Penal Code 502 is adequately pled, and the cross-complaint sufficiently alleges that the Executives engaged in unreasonable and wrongful conduct to create personal liability. The common law misappropriation and interference with prospective economic advantage causes of action are based on the same nucleus of facts as the UTSA cause of action. SBM, however, has leave to plead a cause of action for conversion based on the theft of its tangible property. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CROWN BUILDING MAINTENANCE, INC., A BUILDING VS. SBM SITE SERVICES, LLC, AN OREGON LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Stay Action Pursuant To Ccp 410.30(A)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 7, 2011, Line 9. PLAINTIFF CROWN BUILDING MAINTENANCE, INC.'S NOTICE AND MOTION TO STAY ACTION PURSUANT TO CCP 4l0.30(A). Denied. Assuming without deciding that a party who initiates a lawsuit in a California court after a related lawsuit was underway in an assertedly suitable forum is not estopped from bringing a motion to stay the lawsuit it filed in favor of the prior action, plaintiff has not shown that this court is an inconvenient forum. Moveover, the public and private factors do not support a stay. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SAL ROSSELLI et al VS. SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Waiver Of Undertaking
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 7, 2011, Line 11. PLAINTIFFS SAL ROSSELLI, JOHN BORSOS, RALPH CORNEJO, BEVERLY GRIFFITH, JAN HARRIS, IRMA DUFELMEIER AND ANGELA GLASPER'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR WAIVER OF UNDERTAKING. Granted in part and denied in part. Relief from the undertaking requirement is denied. (See Dowling v. Zimmerman (2001) 85 Cal.App.4th 1400, 1432). Enforcement of the fee order is stayed until December 12, 2011 to allow plaintiff time to obtain a bond. Defendant to receive $2500 as reasonable attorney¿s fees for opposing this simple motion. rulings.Case Title: CAROL L. PRICE VS. ROBERT E. WHITE
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 7, 2011, Line 12. DEFENDANT ROBERT WHITE'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Duplicate entry. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CAROL L. PRICE VS. ROBERT E. WHITE
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 7, 2011, Line 12. DEFENDANT ROBERT WHITE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff acknowledge that she discovered the alleged wrongful conduct in November 2009, yet she filed this action on May 12, 2011, rendering it time-barred. (CCP 340.6(a).) The statute of limitations for legal malpractice is not tolled by the arbitration of attorney fees disputes. (Bus. & Prof. Code 6200(b)(2).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: SHAMROCK STRUCTURES, INC. VS. BANYA 2000 LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 7, 2011, Line 13. PLAINTIFF SHAMROCK STRUCTURES, INC.'S NOTICE OF HEARING ON PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. Denied. The Federal Arbitration Act applies because the parties¿ contract evidences a transaction in commerce. Defendants have shown that plaintiff knew of its existing right to compel arbitration, plaintiff acted inconsistently with that right by filing and proceeding with this lawsuit, and defendants have been prejudiced by the delay in seeking arbitration because they have already spent considerable time and money in litigation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
